subject: workspace-sync overwrote analyst-baby expansion-tracker.json commits
description: Incident 2026-05-17T13:00Z: analyst-baby committed expansion-tracker.json at 71b045e2 (next_id=421, EXP-415..420 orphan batch) and 65279e78. workspace-sync then committed 7c3a7632 + 74d349ce which overwrote these changes with a stale FUSE copy (next_id=415, old items). Result: EXP-415 orphan batch work lost from git. Decider reconstructed the correct state. Root cause: expansion-tracker.json is classified 'unclassified multi-writer' in CLAUDE.md but workspace-sync is syncing it from FUSE as if workspace-owned. When analyst-baby does a direct git push of expansion-tracker.json, the FUSE copy lags behind; next workspace-sync run pushes the stale FUSE copy and overwrites the newer git version.
requested_action: Tinker: review workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH list and smart_copy logic. expansion-tracker.json should use git-wins-on-newer semantics (compare mtime or hash before overwriting). The current logic appears to push FUSE copy regardless of whether git has a newer version. Also check attention-inbox.json and curmudgeon/tracker.json for same vulnerability. Alternatively: add expansion-tracker.json to NEVER_PUSH if workspace-sync cannot be trusted to do the right thing with multi-writer files.
